branch.action: Announce: "✅ E2E Regression Suite: [N] tests passed. Feature '[Name]' locked in."
Set phase_4_substep="e2e_passed". Proceed to Step 4.
Determine failure type:
branch.sub_branch: Re-spawn E2E generation subtask with failure output to fix the test. Do NOT modify application code.
True regression — new feature broke locked-in behavior. Spawn code subtask to fix application code. Do NOT modify the E2E test.
Fix application code first, then re-spawn QA if needed.
branch.action: Wait for fix, re-run suite.
Present to user with options: "Retry", "Skip E2E lock-in (not recommended)", "I'll fix manually". If skipped: document in current-state.md as "⚠️ Feature not locked into E2E regression suite."
Announce lock-in. Set phase_4_substep="e2e_passed". Proceed to Step 4.

step.title: Architectural Alignment Audit
step.action: Check if all 4 knowledge files exist in agent-workspace/knowledge/. If any are missing, see knowledge_files_do_not_exist in error_handling. If present, read all four.

audit_checklist.item: Spaghetti Code: circular dependencies or tightly coupled components?
Duplicated Logic: same patterns described in multiple places without shared abstraction?
Architectural Drift: new feature deviate from the planning/ directory's established architecture?
Wave Integrity: does this feature properly extend Wave N abstractions rather than modifying them?
Tech Debt Signals: TODO markers, "temporary" solutions, bypass patterns?
Knowledge Completeness: all four files populated with meaningful content?
Test Coverage Visibility: documentation reference tests/unit/ and tests/e2e/ coverage?

step.action: Assign Architectural Health rating (Excellent/Good/Fair/Poor) with specific evidence.
Set phase_4_substep="audited" in state.json.

step.title: Vision Re-evaluation — Keep the Vision Fluid
step.description: Compare what was just built against the vision documents. The vision is fluid — it must always reflect the true current understanding of the final app.
step.action: Read all vision docs in agent-workspace/project-director/vision/.

Check the FD's completion summary for the scope-drift assessment. If the delivered feature diverges from the acceptance criteria derived by the FD Architect, note which criteria were affected and whether the adjustment was minor or major. This tells you where to focus during the vision comparison below.

Roadmap alignment check: read the feature's entry from roadmap.md. Compare the acceptance criteria from the FD's archived plan against four sources: the FD's completion summary, the git diff (try to re-run `git diff --stat trunk...feature-branch` if the diff from Phase 3 Step 4 check (0) is stale or was never generated), the knowledge file updates, and the actual project files. If the git diff is completely unavailable (git disabled, branch null, or command fails): fall back to using knowledge/architecture.md for the structural file inventory and the FD's archived plan at agent-workspace/feature/archive/plan-[sanitized].md for the per-step file list — use these to reconstruct what files should exist and cross-reference against the project. When the diff IS available: use its file list as your systematic map — instead of spot-checking random files, use it to guide targeted reads of every modified directory. Pay special attention to: (i) Wave 1-2 abstraction files appearing in the diff — these should almost never be modified by Wave 3 features, (ii) directories that don't match the feature's domain, (iii) new shared utilities or config changes not mentioned in the roadmap. Do not attempt to verify code correctness (that was QA's job) — verify structural alignment: do the right files exist? Do they reference the expected patterns?

Compare against: FD's completion summary (including your independent verification from Phase 3 Step 4), the feature's roadmap entry, and what was actually built (from knowledge files, audit findings, and project file spot-checks).

branch.condition: NO DRIFT — built feature matches vision exactly
branch.action: No changes to vision docs. Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": "Feature [N] built as envisioned — no vision changes."

branch.condition: MINOR DRIFT — small discrepancies that don't affect other features
branch.action: PD edits the relevant vision .md file(s) directly to reflect reality. Examples: layout changed from 3-column to 2-column, added a button not in vision, feature behavior slightly simpler than described.
Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": what changed and why.

branch.condition: MAJOR DRIFT — changes that affect remaining roadmap features
branch.action: Flag for user via ask_followup_question: "The vision and what we built have diverged. [Describe the specific divergence and which remaining features it affects.] How should we proceed?" Provide options: 1. "Re-plan remaining features with updated vision" 2. "Accept the divergence — update vision docs but don't re-plan" 3. "I'll manually update the vision and roadmap"
Set phase_4_substep="replan_in_progress" in state.json (crash guard: if context is lost during re-plan, resume knows Architect was spawned).
Spawn Architect with: updated vision docs, list of completed features (1 through current_feature_index), and instruction to revise roadmap for remaining features only.

After Architect returns: VALIDATE that new total_features >= current_feature_index. If new total_features < current_feature_index: flag to user via ask_followup_question — "The re-plan removed [N] features that were already built (total_features dropped from [old] to [new], but current_feature_index is [index]). How proceed?" Options: "Restore removed features to roadmap", "Mark project complete with remaining features as backlog", "I will manually adjust the roadmap." Do NOT proceed until resolved.

Perform a generalization audit on the new/revised features: scan for over-specific vendor references, ensure all integration points use abstraction patterns. If issues found, re-spawn Architect with specific revision targets (same as Phase 2 Step 7). If Architect fails after a second attempt: escalate to user with documented risk note. Present the remaining over-specific features and let the user choose: "Accept with risk note", "I will provide manual guidance", or "Restart execution planning from scratch."
Present the revised roadmap for user approval using the same gate format as Phase 2 Step 4 (summary, 4 response options, human approval required). Do NOT proceed until approved.

Only AFTER user approves the revised roadmap: update total_features in state.json if count changed. Increment total_replans in state.json by 1. If user rejects (asks for revision or manual edits): do NOT update total_features or total_replans — handle the revision/review path without mutating state, then re-present for approval. Only mutate state.json after final approval.
PD edits vision docs to match reality. Document gap in current-state.md.
Wait for user. When ready, re-read vision docs and roadmap. Update total_features in state.json if changed.

step.action: Set phase_4_substep="vision_reevaluated" in state.json.

step.title: Update the Current State Dashboard
step.action: Verify current-state.md exists on disk before reading. If missing: recreate from the dashboard template in common patterns (same as Phase 1 Step 2 fresh project path). The dashboard content will be repopulated during this step's updates. Announce: "current-state.md was missing — recreated from template."
Read current-state.md and update:

update_sections.section: Append 2-4 bullet points for the new feature's capabilities.
Update UI layout based on FD's report and frontend-ui.md.
Replace with new Health rating and bullet-point findings.
Document any vision changes from Step 5 (what changed, why, re-plan status).
Add/update: total E2E tests, framework used, run command, list of locked-in features, any features not locked in (with reason).

step.action: Set phase_4_substep="dashboarded" in state.json.

Commit Phase 4 changes. Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If true: Ensure the following Phase 4 artifacts are committed: the new E2E test file in tests/e2e/, the updated current-state.md, and any modified vision docs in agent-workspace/project-director/vision/. Use a commit message summarizing the QA results: feature number, unit test outcome, E2E lock-in status, audit rating, and whether dashboard/vision were updated. If false: announce "Phase 4 changes ready. Please manually commit: tests/e2e/, current-state.md, vision/, and any regression fixes."

step.title: Set Phase State
step.action: Set current_phase=5 in state.json.

step.title: Present the Completion Report
step.action: Present a concise report: feature name/number, wave, branch, unit test results, E2E lock-in status (framework + test file), Architectural Health rating with evidence, vision changes, tech debt items.

step.title: The PR Offer
step.action: Read git_automation_enabled from state.json.

Use ask_followup_question: "Feature [Name] complete on branch [branch]. Unit tests passed. E2E: [locked ✅ / not locked ⚠️]. Architectural health: [Rating]. Vision: [no changes / minor updates / major re-plan]. How proceed?"
Provide exactly 4 responses: 1. "Push branch and create PR for me (uses GitHub CLI)" 2. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Proceed to next feature." 3. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Inject a refactoring feature." 4. "Hold on, I need to make manual changes."
Provide exactly 3 responses: 1. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Proceed to next feature." 2. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Inject a refactoring feature." 3. "Hold on, I need to make manual changes." Note: Option 1 (auto-PR) is hidden because git automation is disabled.
step.warning: Options 2 and 3 will delete the local feature branch. Ensure the PR has been merged before proceeding.

decision_tree.branch: Proceed to Step 4 (Post-Merge Cleanup). Refactoring injection happens in Step 5.
Wait for user. When ready, re-present the PR Offer.

step.title: Handle PR Creation (if selected)
step.trigger: User selects "Push branch and create PR for me"
step.action: Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If false: skip. Announce "Git automation disabled. Please push and create PR manually." and present post-PR options.

sub_step.action: Push the feature branch to the remote tracking branch. If the push fails, report error to user per error_handling pr_creation_fails scenario.

Create a pull request for the feature branch with title "Feature: [Name]" and a body summarizing test results, architectural audit rating, and vision status. If PR creation tooling is not available, report error per error_handling.
Present PR URL. Use ask_followup_question with 2 responses: 1. "Merged — proceed to next feature" 2. "Merged — inject refactoring feature next"

step.title: Post-Merge Cleanup & Verification
step.action: Before cleanup, read current_branch_name from state.json. If current_branch_name is null (FD skipped git), skip all cleanup and announce: "No feature branch to clean up. Proceeding."
Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If false: skip all cleanup, announce "Git automation disabled. Please clean up branches manually."

sub_step.action: Identify the trunk branch (main, master, develop, or trunk — whichever exists). Checkout the trunk and pull the latest changes.
Delete the feature branch locally.
Verify the current branch is the trunk branch.

step.action: Announce: "Cleanup complete. On branch [current-branch]. Ready for next feature."

step.title: Course Correction & State Update
step.action:
If user selected to inject a refactoring feature: edit roadmap.md following these rules:
Insert at position current_feature_index + 1. Increment subsequent feature numbers. Name descriptively based on audit findings (e.g., "Feature [N]: Refactoring — Extract Shared Auth Hook"). Place in same wave unless refactoring spans entire codebase. Cite specific evidence from architectural audit. Increment total_features in state.json by 1.
If scope-creep ideas were mentioned: add to backlog.md with date and proposed-by.
Update state.json: increment current_feature_index by 1, set fd_status="idle", phase_4_substep=null, current_branch_name=null. Keep current_phase=3, status="in_progress".
Loop back to Phase 3 Step 1 for the next feature.

completion_criteria.criterion: All features in the roadmap built and merged (current_feature_index == total_features).
All unit tests pass for the complete project.
All E2E regression tests pass. Any feature not locked in documented in current-state.md with reason.
current-state.md dashboard reflects all completed features, UI map, vision updates, and E2E regression suite status.
Vision docs accurately reflect the true built state of the app (all minor drifts reconciled).
Architectural health assessment is "Good" or better, or all tech debt items documented in backlog.md.
All feature branches cleaned up locally.
